span.p: New or reset project — how to run it?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Are requirements stable and well-understood up front?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Fixed scope+date+budget OR a stage-gate/governance mandate?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Is scope OR date contractually fixed?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
PREDICTIVE (delivery-lead)
charter + WBS + baseline + change control + earned value

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
HYBRID — predictive frame, agile delivery
baseline the milestones, run the build in sprints

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
AGILE (scrum-master)
backlog + sprints/flow + velocity + empirical replanning

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
HYBRID
fixed outer baseline + change control,
agile increments inside; reconcile each cycle

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Cadenced deliverables, or continuous interrupt-driven work?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Kanban — WIP limits + flow metrics, no sprint
